gazette: The Official Journal of the European Union OJ L 159I , 23.6.2023
title: The changes regarding the EU's sanctions on Russia and the steel sector
bodyHtml:
The Council of the European Union has approved a new package of sanctions to strengthen the existing measures against Russia and prevent circumvention.

Under the new sanction package, restrictions on the import of iron and steel products are being tightened. Importers will be required to provide evidence that the iron and steel inputs used in processed goods subject to sanctions do not originate from Russia.

According to the amendment published in the Official Journal of the European Union on June 23, the import of iron and steel products listed in Annex XVII of Regulation 833/1214 will require the presentation of a proof of origin demonstrating that the inputs used in the production of these goods are not of Russian origin (Article 1, Paragraph 12 of the Regulation).

The changes will come into effect from September 30, 2023.

The product codes covered by these measures can be found in Annex V on page 229.

For the list of covered products and the amended regulation, please click here.
